title: In This Book
content:
In this book I have scribbled some innocent rhymes,
    In various moods, and at different times;
    Some grave and some cheerful, some merry, some sad,
    Though none may be good, there are none very bad.
meaning:
Eliza Paul Kirkbride Gurney's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "In This Book"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
